assertions.title: Overwrite Existing Preset

steps.action: Create preset g2-overwrite-test with query 'SELECT original_query'
Attempt preset add with same name
steps.expected: Error PRESET_EXISTS with hint to use edit

steps.action: Use preset edit to update query and description
Verify preset show reflects changes
steps.observed: Query='SELECT replaced_query WHERE id = :id', description='Replaced preset'

evidence.cliOutput: preset edit returned fields_updated and new updatedAt timestamp
evidence.jsonVerification: preset show confirmed query and description were replaced

assertions.notes: preset add does not allow overwriting (returns PRESET_EXISTS error) - this is good UX. preset edit successfully updates specified fields and refreshes updatedAt.

steps.action: Create preset g2-preserve-a with query 'SELECT preset_a'
Create preset g2-preserve-b with query 'SELECT preset_b'
Edit preset g2-preserve-a with new query and description
Verify preset g2-preserve-b unchanged
steps.observed: query='SELECT preset_b', description='Preset B - should not change when A is edited', updatedAt unchanged

evidence.cliOutput: preset show g2-preserve-b confirmed unchanged values
evidence.storageVerification: presets.json showed both presets with correct data

steps.action: Create preset g2-active-test
Use preset use g2-active-test
Check presets.json for activePreset field
Run preset use (no args) in new CLI session

evidence.cliOutput: preset use returned active preset correctly after new session

steps.action: Preset g2-active-test was already active from previous test
Remove g2-active-test with --force
steps.expected: Preset removed, was_active: true in response

steps.action: Check active preset status

evidence.cliOutput: preset remove returned was_active: true; preset use returned active_preset: null

assertions.title: Atomic File Write

steps.action: Rapid sequential create+edit operations for 5 presets
steps.observed: All 5 presets created and edited successfully

steps.action: Verify presets.json is valid JSON
Check for leftover temp files in config directory
steps.observed: Only presets.json exists, no temp files

evidence.cliOutput: All rapid write operations completed successfully
evidence.fileVerification: JSON is valid, no temp files in config directory

assertions.notes: Cannot directly verify atomic write implementation through CLI, but evidence suggests it's working: no corrupted JSON after rapid operations, no temp files left behind.

steps.action: Read presets.json file
Verify not minified

assertions.title: Empty Presets File Valid State

steps.action: Backup and delete presets.json
Run preset list without file existing
Add first preset to trigger file creation
steps.observed: File created with valid JSON containing the preset

steps.action: Restore backup

evidence.cliOutput: preset list handles missing file gracefully (returns empty list)
evidence.fileVerification: File created with valid JSON structure when first preset added

assertions.notes: Load operation handles missing file gracefully. File is created on-demand when first preset is added with valid JSON structure.

assertions.title: Unicode and Special Characters Support

steps.action: Create preset with Unicode: Ñoño, 日本語, Greek, Hebrew, emoji 🎉, café, special SQL chars
Show preset and verify Unicode preserved
steps.observed: Ñoño, 日本語, Ελληνικά, עברית, 🎉, café all preserved in output

steps.action: Verify special SQL characters (single quotes) escaped
steps.observed: Single quotes doubled ('') for SQL safety

evidence.evidenceFile: evidence/preset-crud/flow-storage-persist/VAL-STORAGE-015-unicode-output.json (note: PowerShell encoding issues in file, but CLI output was correct)

assertions.notes: PowerShell file encoding caused garbled characters in evidence file, but CLI output confirmed Unicode is preserved correctly in storage.

frictions.description: PowerShell's Out-File has encoding issues with Unicode characters, causing evidence files to show garbled text even though CLI output is correct
frictions.resolution: Noted that CLI output shows correct Unicode; evidence file encoding issue is a PowerShell limitation, not a CLI bug

summary: Tested 8 assertions for storage persistence: all passed. Key findings: (1) preset add prevents accidental overwrites (good UX), preset edit updates specified fields; (2) active preset persists correctly and clears on deletion; (3) atomic writes appear working (no corruption, no temp files); (4) Unicode preserved correctly in storage.
